Notes: Most common E. coli origin. Found in pUC19 pBluescript pGEM. High copy ~500 with rop deletion. Requires RNAII primer and RNAI inhibitor.
pMB1 derivative of ColE1. Found in all pUC vectors. rop gene deleted → very high copy. Identical functional region to ColE1.
Compatible with ColE1 vectors. Found in pACYC184 pZA vectors. Useful for dual-plasmid systems. Medium copy number.
Stringent low-copy origin. Requires pSC101-encoded RepA protein. Used when tight gene dosage control needed.
Filamentous phage f1 origin. Enables ssDNA rescue using M13KO7 helper phage. Found in pBluescript pGEX pUC118.
SV40 T-antigen origin. Episomal replication in COS cells expressing large T antigen. Found in pcDNA3.1 pSV2 vectors.
S. cerevisiae 2-micron plasmid origin. ~25-30 copies per cell. Requires REP1 REP2 FLP functions.
Centromeric plus autonomously replicating sequence. 1-2 copies per cell. Stable segregation like chromosomes.
Epstein-Barr virus latent origin. Requires EBNA-1 protein. Episomal maintenance in EBNA-1 expressing cells.
Standard pUC19 origin. Same as pMB1 (ColE1 family). NCBI L09137 contains complete pUC19 sequence (2686 bp).
